memory.summary: I have a free afternoon with enough energy to complete the scholarship application, which offers significant financial benefit and requires focused effort. While a fun activity is available now, the deadline-driven nature of the scholarship makes it the higher priority task.
Starting this scholarship application now creates slack for unexpected delays, making it an adjustable-cost optimization rather than a high-stress deadline rush.

My scholarship application is now complete, but with zero energy and significant sleep debt, I must stop working immediately to recover.

goals.description: Complete the short essay and upload two documents for the scholarship application.
goals.rationale: The deadline is in 3 days with ~95% progress; finishing this task requires a dedicated block of time before it becomes urgent, whereas the fun option has a distant deadline on Monday.

agent.last_reasoning: The application is complete (100% progress). Since the fun option has a distant Monday deadline and I have zero energy, attending now restores energy without risking the scholarship. This balances immediate recovery with future obligations.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
